reasoning: After 7 searches, I've found no evidence suggesting a plausible mechanism for 11+ Battle events in Antarctica within any 30-day period. Key findings: (1) Antarctica has no permanent population or military forces (2) Antarctic Treaty System (1959) prohibits military activity and has successfully maintained peace (3) Historical 'Battles' reference value of 1.000 suggests near-zero baseline (4) ACLED database access requires registration but no public sources show actual Antarctica Battle events (5) Even illegal fishing conflicts, while violent in other regions like Argentina, are not documented as reaching Battle threshold in Antarctica waters under ACLED coding. Without catastrophic treaty collapse or major geopolitical disruption, the probability remains vanishingly small across all forecast horizons. No temporal decay needed as the structural constraints are permanent.
